rows.signal: admin guard takes tenant binding as optional arg; data layer scopes by the path companyId
rows.why: an admin in tenant A received tenant B's distinct company data, member PII and chat lists across multiple routes, while the bound sibling denied the same cross-tenant request

controls.negative: the binding-enforcing sibling (company-settings) returned 403 for the same cross-tenant id
controls.positive: attacker's own-tenant request returned 200 with the attacker's own company (detector works)
controls.canary: victim company display name, distinct from the attacker's company name
controls.replay: re-run in a freshly minted session/token; stable

rows.signal: teams list/create route under authn-only; no role guard, no tenant bind
rows.why: a non-admin in one tenant listed another tenant's teams (200 with that tenant's distinct team names), and an admin created then deleted a team inside another tenant

controls.negative: dashboard route returned 403 for the same non-admin (role gate exists elsewhere); company-settings returned 403 cross-tenant
controls.positive: own-tenant teams list returned 200 (baseline); created team appeared in the victim tenant set then was absent after delete
controls.canary: labeled throwaway team name in the victim tenant; victim tenant team count incremented then restored
controls.replay: stable across fresh tokens; write reverted and verified absent

rows.signal: non-admin attempting a cross-tenant admin dashboard read
rows.why: the role gate fired and returned 403 even cross-tenant — the missing control was the tenant bind, not the role check; do not conflate the two

controls.negative: same non-admin got 403 on own-tenant dashboard too (role gate, not tenancy)
controls.positive: the same non-admin DID succeed on the no-role teams route — isolating that this refutation is specific to role-gated routes
